titleStmt.title: Letter from Séan P Campbell to Hanna Sheehy Skeffington, 10 November 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Séan P. Campbell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Séan P Campbell (1889-1950) to Hanna Sheehy Skeffington (1977-1946) regarding the upcoming Anti-Conscription Committee meeting and the publication of their pamphlet.
Séan P Campbell was an Irish senator and Sinn Fein supporter. He acted as honorary secretary of both the Sinn Fein central branch and the Anti-Conscription Committee in 1915. He was a advocate for the development of the Irish industry.
Hanna Sheehy Skeffington was a suffragette, nationalist, language teacher, was the founder of the Irish Women’s Franchise League and a founding member of the Irish Women Workers’ Union. She was the widow of Francis Sheehy-Skeffington who was summarily executed on 26 April 1916.
She was active during the Rising, bringing food to the Volunteers in the G.P.O. and the College of Surgeons. Four days passed before she found out what had happened to her husband, Francis (1878-1916), and it wasn't until almost two weeks later that the full details of his execution emerged.

msIdentifier.collection: Sheehy-Skeffington Papers, Collection List No. 47; MS 33,604/13-1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
ANTI-CONSCRIPTION COMMITTEE,
41 YORK STREET,
DUBLIN, 10 — XI 1915. Dear Mrs. Skeffington I beg to acknowledge receipt ofyour letter of
the 9th inst. The Committee have not yet fixed on
any date for the holding of the Anti-Conscription
Meeting, but they hope to definitely settle the matter at
next Tuesday's meeting when I trust you may be
able to be present. In reference to the pamphlet no definite date for
its publication has been settled upon, and this matter
will also be dealt with at next meeting. We did not transact very much business last night
as there were only two or three members present. I am,
Faithfully Yours, Seán P. Campbell,
Hon.Sec. Mrs. H.S. Skeffington,
11 Grosvenor Place
Rathmines
